text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
ANATOMIC PATHOLOGY REPORT

================================================================================
PATIENT: Margaret Yuen
CLINICAL SERVICE: Thoracic Oncology / Interventional Pulmonology
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
SPECIMEN TYPE: CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy (Right Upper Lobe Mass) AND Endobronchial Ultrasound-Guided Fine Needle Aspiration (Right Paratracheal Lymph Node, Station 4R)
CLINICAL HISTORY: 62-year-old female with a 20 pack-year smoking history (former smoker, quit 12 years prior), presenting with progressive cough and dyspnea. CT/PET-CT imaging demonstrated a 4.8 cm hypermetabolic spiculated right upper lobe mass with ipsilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy involving stations 4R and 7. Staging biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis and molecular characterization.
CLINICAL DIAGNOSIS (SUBMITTING PROVIDER): Right upper lobe mass, rule out primary lung malignancy.
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
FROZEN SECTION: Not performed.
================================================================================

PROCEDURE DATE: [Procedure Date]
REPORT DATE: [Report Date]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Part A: CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass (18-gauge, 3 cores submitted in formalin)
Part B: EBUS-guided fine needle aspiration, right paratracheal lymph node, station 4R (cell block and smear preparations)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Part A (CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass):
Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "right upper lobe core biopsy," are three tan-white to pale gray cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments measuring in aggregate 1.4 cm in greatest dimension. The largest individual core measures 0.7 x 0.2 x 0.2 cm; the smallest measures 0.3 x 0.1 x 0.1 cm. The cores are firm to slightly rubbery in consistency. Cut surfaces reveal homogeneous tan-white tissue without grossly identifiable necrosis or hemorrhage. All three cores are submitted entirely in one cassette after placement in HistoGel embedding medium.

Representative sections: All tissue submitted. 1 cassette (A1).

Part B (EBUS-FNA, right paratracheal lymph node, station 4R):
Received are three Papanicolaou-stained smear preparations, three Diff-Quik-stained smear preparations, and one formalin-fixed cell block preparation, all labeled with the patient's name and "station 4R FNA." The smears are adequately cellular. The cell block is processed into a paraffin-embedded pellet measuring 0.5 x 0.3 x 0.2 cm. Representative sections from the cell block are submitted in one cassette (B1). Smear preparations retained for correlation.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Part A (CT-guided core needle biopsy, right upper lobe mass):
Histologic sections of the core needle biopsy demonstrate a cellular neoplasm infiltrating a desmoplastic stroma. The tumor is composed of atypical epithelial cells arranged predominantly in irregular glandular and acinar formations (acinar-predominant pattern per IASLC/ATS/ERS 2015 classification). Focally, neoplastic cells are arranged along intact alveolar septa in a lepidic growth pattern, comprising an estimated 15-20% of the tumor. The neoplastic cells exhibit moderate nuclear pleomorphism, with round to oval nuclei containing prominent nucleoli, finely granular chromatin, and moderate amounts of pale eosinophilic to amphophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are present, estimated at 4 per 10 high-power fields. No definitive vascular or lymphatic space invasion is identified within the biopsy. No necrosis is identified. The background lung parenchyma shows mild anthracotic pigment deposition and minimal emphysematous changes consistent with the patient's smoking history.

Part B (EBUS-FNA, right paratracheal lymph node, station 4R):
Smear preparations and cell block sections from the right paratracheal lymph node (station 4R) demonstrate numerous clusters and three-dimensional groups of malignant epithelial cells within a background of lymphoid elements and reactive lymphocytes. The malignant cells are morphologically identical to those identified in Part A, exhibiting acinar/glandular arrangements, moderate nuclear pleomorphism, prominent nucleoli, and moderate pale cytoplasm. Scattered single malignant cells and small cohesive clusters are present between sheets of small lymphocytes. The findings are consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma, morphologically indistinguishable from the primary lung adenocarcinoma sampled in Part A. No extracapsular extension can be assessed on FNA material.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Part A, FFPE core biopsy sections; confirmatory stains performed on Part B cell block as indicated):

The following immunohistochemical stains were performed using validated antibody clones on the Leica BOND-III automated platform with appropriate positive and negative controls:

  TTF-1 (SPT24 clone):          POSITIVE -- strong, diffuse nuclear staining in >90% of tumor cells
  Napsin-A (IP64 clone):        POSITIVE -- granular cytoplasmic staining in approximately 70-80% of tumor cells
  CK7 (OV-TL 12/30 clone):     POSITIVE -- strong, diffuse cytoplasmic staining
  CK20 (Ks20.8 clone):         NEGATIVE -- no immunoreactivity in tumor cells
  p40 (BC28 clone):             NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells
  p63 (4A4 clone):              NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells
  Synaptophysin (MRQ-40 clone): NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A (LK2H10 clone):NEGATIVE

Interpretation: The immunoprofile (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+, CK7+, CK20-, p40-, p63-) is consistent with primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma. The findings effectively exclude squamous cell carcinoma, small cell carcinoma, and metastatic adenocarcinoma of gastrointestinal origin.

PD-L1 EXPRESSION ANALYSIS (Part A, FFPE core biopsy):
  Assay: DAKO PD-L1 IHC 22C3 pharmDx (FDA-approved companion diagnostic)
  Platform: Dako Autostainer Link 48
  Tumor Proportion Score (TPS): 15%
  Interpretation: PD-L1 LOW POSITIVE (TPS 1-49%). Membranous PD-L1 staining is identified in approximately 15% of viable tumor cells. Immune cell staining is present but not scored by TPS methodology. Internal positive control tissue present and reactive.

ALK IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Part A, FFPE core biopsy):
  Assay: Ventana ALK (D5F3) CDx Assay (FDA-approved companion diagnostic)
  Result: NEGATIVE -- no granular cytoplasmic staining above background in tumor cells
  Interpretation: ALK rearrangement not detected by IHC. Reflex FISH not required per institutional protocol when D5F3 CDx IHC result is negative.

ROS1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (Part A, FFPE core biopsy):
  Assay: ROS1 IHC (D4D6 clone, Cell Signaling Technology)
  Result: NEGATIVE -- no cytoplasmic staining above background in tumor cells
  Interpretation: ROS1 overexpression not detected. Reflex ROS1 FISH confirmatory testing performed per institutional protocol given the requirement for FISH confirmation of ROS1-negative IHC results (see below).

ROS1 FISH (Part A, FFPE core biopsy; performed as reflexive confirmation):
  Probe: Vysis ROS1 Break-Apart FISH Probe
  Result: NEGATIVE -- no split signal pattern identified; >90% of nuclei show intact (fused) signal pattern
  Interpretation: ROS1 gene rearrangement not detected by FISH.

EGFR MUTATION TESTING -- COBAS PCR (Part A, FFPE core biopsy):
  Assay: cobas EGFR Mutation Test v2 (Roche Molecular Systems; FDA-approved companion diagnostic)
  Result: POSITIVE -- EGFR Exon 21 L858R point mutation detected
  No EGFR exon 19 deletion detected.
  No EGFR exon 20 insertion detected.
  No EGFR T790M (exon 20) detected.
  Interpretation: EGFR sensitizing mutation (L858R, exon 21) identified. This mutation is associated with sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy. Case has been reflexed to comprehensive next-generation sequencing (NGS) panel per institutional protocol for complete molecular characterization prior to treatment planning (NGS report to follow under separate accession).

NOTE -- STUDIES PENDING:
  Comprehensive NGS profiling (solid tumor panel): Specimen submitted; results PENDING. Separate report will be issued when results are finalized. Expected turnaround: 10-14 business days.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Part A: CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY, RIGHT UPPER LOBE MASS:
  INVASIVE PULMONARY ADENOCARCINOMA, ACINAR-PREDOMINANT PATTERN WITH FOCAL
  LEPIDIC COMPONENT (estimated lepidic component: 15-20%).
  -- TTF-1 positive (strong, diffuse); Napsin-A positive; CK7 positive;
     CK20 negative; p40 negative; p63 negative.
  -- PD-L1 TPS: 15% (22C3 pharmDx assay; LOW POSITIVE).
  -- ALK D5F3 CDx IHC: NEGATIVE.
  -- ROS1 IHC (D4D6): NEGATIVE; confirmed by FISH: NEGATIVE.
  -- EGFR cobas PCR: L858R DETECTED (exon 21); no exon 19 deletion, no T790M detected.
  -- COMPREHENSIVE NGS: PENDING (separate report to follow).
  -- No necrosis identified.
  -- No definitive angiolymphatic invasion identified in biopsy material.

Part B: EBUS-FNA, RIGHT PARATRACHEAL LYMPH NODE, STATION 4R:
  METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, MORPHOLOGICALLY CONSISTENT WITH PULMONARY PRIMARY
  (see Part A).
  -- Malignant epithelial cells within a background of reactive lymphoid elements.
  -- Morphology identical to primary tumor in Part A.
  -- Confirmatory IHC (TTF-1) performed on cell block: POSITIVE.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The histomorphologic and immunohistochemical findings confirm invasive pulmonary adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe, acinar-predominant with focal lepidic features, consistent with a primary lung origin. The concurrent EBUS-FNA of the right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node demonstrates nodal involvement by metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with N2 nodal disease in the context of the clinical and radiologic staging information provided.

The EGFR exon 21 L858R sensitizing mutation has been detected by cobas PCR and is a validated predictive biomarker. ALK rearrangement and ROS1 rearrangement have been excluded by validated IHC and FISH methods. PD-L1 TPS of 15% (22C3) indicates low positive expression. Comprehensive NGS profiling is reflexed and pending; results will be reported separately and should be reviewed in conjunction with this report for complete molecular tumor characterization prior to treatment planning.

This case was reviewed at the thoracic multidisciplinary tumor board conference.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGNATURE: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

This report has been electronically signed.
================================================================================
END OF REPORT
================================================================================
